pattern: Greedy: always make the most expensive cut first; each cut's effective cost multiplies by how many parallel pieces it traverses.
hints: When a cut is made, it applies to all parallel sub-pieces that span that line — so a horizontal cut at line i costs horizontalCut[i] multiplied by the number of vertical segments currently present.
Sort horizontal and vertical costs in descending order, then greedily pick the larger remaining cut.
Track how many horizontal and vertical pieces exist; use those as multipliers for each cut chosen.

edge_inputs.description: All horizontal costs > vertical — always pick horizontal first
All vertical costs > horizontal — always pick vertical first

stress.description: Random m,n in [2..20] with random cut costs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(2, size)
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    hcut = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(m - 1)]
    vcut = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n - 1)]
    return [m, n, hcut, vcut]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minimumCost(self, m, n, horizontalCut, verticalCut):
        hcuts = sorted(horizontalCut, reverse=True)
        vcuts = sorted(verticalCut, reverse=True)
        h_pieces = 1
        v_pieces = 1
        hi = 0
        vi = 0
        total = 0
        while hi < len(hcuts) and vi < len(vcuts):
            if hcuts[hi] >= vcuts[vi]:
                total += hcuts[hi] * v_pieces
                h_pieces += 1
                hi += 1
            else:
                total += vcuts[vi] * h_pieces
                v_pieces += 1
                vi += 1
        while hi < len(hcuts):
            total += hcuts[hi] * v_pieces
            h_pieces += 1
            hi += 1
        while vi < len(vcuts):
            total += vcuts[vi] * h_pieces
            v_pieces += 1
            vi += 1
        return total

complexity.time: O(m log m + n log n)
